title: cgroup_manager config option regression
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

I think I've found a regression between `1.4.0` and `1.4.2` regarding the `cgroup_manager` option in `libpod.conf`. Based on some quick digging podman `1.4.2` does not seem to respect the option set in the configuration file but still respects the cli flag: `--cgroup-manager=cgroupfs`.

Compare two builds of a podman container image that I am working on:

* https://circleci.com/gh/marshallford/podman/14
* https://circleci.com/gh/marshallford/podman/15

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

On a system with podman `1.4.2` that only supports `cgroupfs` do the following:

1. Change `libpod.conf` to read `cgroup_manager = "cgroupfs"`.

2. Run `podman run --rm alpine echo hello`

3. Run `podman run --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs --rm alpine echo hello`

You would expect the outputs to be the same, or am I off base?

**Describe the results you received:**

Ignore the journald errors, yes I am aware of the config option to write events to a file.

```
➜ make test
docker run --rm --privileged -v /var/lib/containers marshallford/podman:`git rev-parse --short HEAD` \
run --rm alpine echo hello from alpine in podman container
journal error: could not connect to journald socket
time="2019-06-24T03:36:47Z" level=error msg="unable to write system event: "journal error: could not connect to journald socket"" 
Trying to pull docker.io/library/alpine...Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:921b31ab772b38172fd9f942a40fae6db24decbd6706f67836260d47a72baab5
Copying config sha256:4d90542f0623c71f1f9c11be3da23167174ac9d93731cf91912922e916bab02c
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
journal error: could not connect to journald socket
journal error: could not connect to journald socket
time="2019-06-24T03:36:51Z" level=error msg="unable to write pod event: "journal error: could not connect to journald socket"" 
journal error: could not connect to journald socket
time="2019-06-24T03:36:52Z" level=error msg="unable to write pod event: "journal error: could not connect to journald socket"" 
Error: time="2019-06-24T03:36:51Z" level=error msg="systemd cgroup flag passed, but systemd support for managing cgroups is not available\n"
systemd cgroup flag passed, but systemd support for managing cgroups is not available
: OCI runtime error
make: *** [Makefile:38: test] Error 127
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

```
➜ make test
docker run --rm --privileged -v /var/lib/containers marshallford/podman:`git rev-parse --short HEAD` \
run --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs --rm alpine echo hello from alpine in podman container
journal error: could not connect to journald socket
time="2019-06-24T03:20:37Z" level=error msg="unable to write system event: "journal error: could not connect to journald socket"" 
Trying to pull docker.io/library/alpine...Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:921b31ab772b38172fd9f942a40fae6db24decbd6706f67836260d47a72baab5
Copying config sha256:4d90542f0623c71f1f9c11be3da23167174ac9d93731cf91912922e916bab02c
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
journal error: could not connect to journald socket
journal error: could not connect to journald socket
time="2019-06-24T03:20:41Z" level=error msg="unable to write pod event: "journal error: could not connect to journald socket"" 
journal error: could not connect to journald socket
time="2019-06-24T03:20:42Z" level=error msg="unable to write pod event: "journal error: could not connect to journald socket"" 
journal error: could not connect to journald socket
time="2019-06-24T03:20:42Z" level=error msg="unable to write pod event: "journal error: could not connect to journald socket"" 
journal error: could not connect to journald socket
time="2019-06-24T03:20:42Z" level=error msg="unable to write pod event: "journal error: could not connect to journald socket"" 
hello from alpine in podman container
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Regression between `1.4.0` and `1.4.2` based on my CICD tooling.

**Output of `podman version`:**

See above.

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

N/A

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Podman within Docker 18.09.xx

comments.body:
I've tried locally and `cgroup_manager` from the configuration file seems to be honored correctly.

Could you attach the output running `podman --log-level=debug ...`?

I've ran into this problem both when building podman from source (alpine) and when using the package published to the fedora repos.

CI of "from source" build: https://circleci.com/gh/marshallford/podman/17

Ah my mistake. I placed `libpod.conf` in `/usr/share/containers` instead of `/etc/containers`. Sorry for troubling you.
